key_findings.summary: Collaboration-bus is the filesystem collab directory with protocol.json + 5 JSONL ledgers; defined/created by bin/stampede.sh and summarized by stampede-monitor.sh.
protocol.json defines required fields for collab ledgers: ts, run_id, commander_id, event, item_id, summary, evidence, confidence, source_refs and event mapping per ledger.
stampede-monitor.sh counts JSONL lines for collab/*.jsonl and child-agents.jsonl but does not parse/validate schema; schema drift or truncation can be invisible.
At least one code path initializes child-agents.jsonl with open("w"), violating append-only guidance and risking truncation/data loss during runs.
Workflow sequence includes "adopt" but protocol only defines ledgers up through broadcast; there is no adopts.jsonl or adopt event schema, so adoption is not persistently recorded/visible.
Collab protocol requires source_refs, but results/*.json and bundle schema do not require or validate source_refs; merger/monitor do not enforce traceability.

notes: This atom is review-only and contains no source changes; recommendations are for follow-up tasks.
Caution: this squad lead session may have reset commander-002/child-agents.jsonl at start; treat earlier telemetry (before 18:18) as potentially lost for commander-002.

recommended_invariants.rule: All ledgers (*.jsonl) must be opened with mode "a" (never "w"); writers must append exactly one json.dumps object + "\n" per record.
All JSON state files must be written via tmp + os.replace (or os.replace directly) to avoid partial writes (swarm-state.json, protocol.json, bundle.json, results/*.json, fleet.json).
Each line in collab ledgers must parse as JSON and contain all required_fields per protocol.json; event must match its ledger mapping.
If workflow includes adopt, create an explicit adoption record (ledger + event) and require bundles/results to reference adopted consensus item_ids.

scope: review-only QA: collaboration-bus + ledgers + atomic semantics + adoption workflow + bundle/result source_refs
